Pseudonymise a vector of IDs

Source: R/privacy.R
bs_pseudonymise_id.Rd

Replaces each value with an HMAC-SHA256 pseudonym of the form usr_a3f2b1c8. The same value + key always produces the same pseudonym, so joins and grouping work correctly within a session.

Usage

bs_pseudonymise_id(values, key)

Arguments

values

A vector of IDs (integer, numeric, or character). NA values are preserved.

key

A raw vector used as the HMAC key. Generate one per session with openssl::rand_bytes(32). Required — there is no default, to force deliberate key management.

Value

A character vector the same length as values, with non-NA entries replaced by usr_ plus 8 hex characters.

Examples

key <- openssl::rand_bytes(32)
bs_pseudonymise_id(c(1, 2, NA, 3), key = key)
#> Error in bs_pseudonymise_id(c(1, 2, NA, 3), key = key): could not find function "bs_pseudonymise_id"
